title: Cache Performance, Scope, Integrity, and Poisoning Defense · Workflow Trust Lens: Ci Cache Poisoning Trust Boundary
summary: A privileged release restores an executable cache written from a fork, accepts broad restore keys, hides cold-install failures, and attributes network time to compilation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci cache poisoning trust boundary increment in cache threat and measurement policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Restrict who can save or restore executable caches across fork, branch, event, workflow, and privilege boundaries.
Construct keys from OS, architecture, tool, dependency lock, relevant configuration, and cache version with bounded restore fallback.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name event, workflow, revision, actor, permissions, runner, toolchain, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, user-signal, and recovery boundaries; predict every state and trust transition; review the smallest deterministic workflow or release change; then verify a fault, repair, rollback, and authorized live-system transfer limit. Apply that move specifically to Ci Cache Poisoning Trust Boundary while producing cache threat and measurement policy.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example privileged release restores executable cache content written by a fork through a broad fallback key. Compare cold and warm time, key scope, save authority, poisoning risk, and immutable artifact separation. Competency probe: Restrict who can save or restore executable caches across fork, branch, event, workflow, and privilege boundaries. A defensible solution applies Ci Cache Poisoning Trust Boundary, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to cache threat and measurement policy.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-cache-poisoning-trust-boundary — Restrict who can save or restore executable caches across fork, branch, event, workflow, and privilege boundaries.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-gha-cache-performance-integrity-3
  - competency: ci-worked-gha-cache-performance-integrity-3
    artifact: cache-policy.yaml
    decision: worked gha cache performance integrity 3
    environment: Node 24 TypeScript monorepo with three lockfiles
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      cache-content: npm-downloads-only
      key: os-arch-node24-lockhash-config
      restore-prefix: exact-trusted-branch
      writer: trusted-push-only
      untrusted-reader: bounded-read
      node-modules: excluded
      privileged-release-cache: disabled
      cold-run: scheduled
      measurement: queue-install-build-test-upload
    verify: compare cold warm and poisoned-key fixtures while checking writer event scope restored key and critical path
    failure: Reject cache as provenance, executable fork-writable caches, broad restore keys, node_modules reuse, or warm-only evidence.
    repair: Cache disposable inputs, bind keys and writers, isolate trust domains, retain cold runs, and measure the actual critical path.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Read-only repository access implies cache writes cannot affect trusted workflows. This fails the Ci Cache Poisoning Trust Boundary contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-cache-poisoning-trust-boundary-18oarb
  - competency: ci-cache-poisoning-trust-boundary
    artifact: cache-policy.yaml
    decision: cache poisoning trust boundary
    environment: protected main branch under a merge queue
    fault-injection: cancel an older run while its external mutation is in progress
    retained-proof: event ref SHA workflow ref workflow SHA run ID and attempt
    case-key: 18oarb
    change: |
      cache-content: npm-downloads-only
      key: os-arch-node24-lockhash-config
      restore-prefix: exact-trusted-branch
      writer: trusted-push-only
      untrusted-reader: bounded-read
      node-modules: excluded
      privileged-release-cache: disabled
      cold-run: scheduled
      measurement: queue-install-build-test-upload
    verify: compare cold warm and poisoned-key fixtures while checking writer event scope restored key and critical path
    failure: Reject cache as provenance, executable fork-writable caches, broad restore keys, node_modules reuse, or warm-only evidence.
    repair: Cache disposable inputs, bind keys and writers, isolate trust domains, retain cold runs, and measure the actual critical path.
